data.title: Japan's Prime Minister Cautions Against Yen Fluctuations Amid Currency Surge
data.description: Japan's Prime Minister has issued a warning regarding unusual movements in the yen, following its most significant rally in six months. According to NS3.AI, this development has led to speculation about potential coordinated intervention with support from the U.S. Federal Reserve. Historically, joint interventions have helped stabilize the yen, weaken the dollar, and enhance global assets, including cryptocurrencies. Although a weaker dollar might favor Bitcoin and other digital assets in the long term, short-term volatility is anticipated as leveraged yen positions are adjusted.

data.title: Bloomberg’s Mike McGlone Warns Bitcoin Faces a “Post-Inflation” Reckoning in 2026
data.description:
Mike McGlone, senior macro strategist at Bloomberg Intelligence, has issued one of his most cautionary outlooks yet on Bitcoin, arguing that the asset is entering a “post-inflation deflation” phase that fundamentally changes its risk profile. After years of maintaining a constructive long-term view, McGlone now advises investors to “sell the rallies” in 2026.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/bloombergs-mike-mcglone-warns-bitcoin-faces-a-post-inflation-reckoning-in-2026/

data.title: Strive's Innovative Strategy to Manage $8 Billion Debt
data.description: Strive, a bitcoin treasury company, is implementing perpetual preferred equity to address its substantial $8 billion in convertible debt. According to NS3.AI, this novel strategy could become a model for handling long-term leverage in cryptocurrency firms. The approach seeks to alleviate debt burdens while maintaining a balance with investor interests.

data.title: FoundryUSA Reduces Hashrate Amid Severe Winter Storm in the U.S.
data.description:
FoundryUSA, a leading digital asset advisory firm with the largest Bitcoin mining pool globally, has significantly reduced its hashrate by approximately 60% since Friday due to a severe winter storm affecting vast areas of the United States. According to Cointelegraph, the Bitcoin hashrate on FoundryUSA alone has decreased by nearly 200 exahashes per second (EH/s), or 60%, since Friday, leading to a temporary slowdown in block production to 12 minutes.

Despite the reduction, FoundryUSA still maintains around 198 EH/s of hashing power, representing about 23% of the global mining pool hashrate, as indicated by data from Hashrate Index. The curtailment has also impacted other mining pools serving U.S. users, including Luxor, as reported by TheMinerMag on Saturday. The winter storm, named Fern, is sweeping across the U.S., prompting miners to adjust their energy consumption to alleviate stress on grid infrastructure.

Bitcoin miners play a crucial role in balancing the energy grid during emergencies. They act as a controllable load resource for public electrical grid infrastructure, modifying their energy requirements to stabilize the grid during periods of high demand and low consumer usage. Excess energy within an electrical grid system can damage infrastructure during low demand periods and must be safely dissipated to prevent harm to grid components and individuals. Bitcoin miners can activate their machines when demand is low, diverting potentially harmful levels of power from the grid. Conversely, they can deactivate their machines during peak demand to ensure energy availability for consumers.

The current winter storm in the U.S. is characterized by a mix of snow, ice, and freezing rain across the Southeastern U.S., the Northeast, and parts of the Midwest, according to radar forecasts from The Weather Channel. The storm is expected to stretch approximately 1,800 miles, causing widespread power outages affecting over 1 million residents, as reported by The Weather Channel.

data.title: Tokenized US Treasuries Exceed $10 Billion in Value
data.description: Tokenized US Treasuries have reached a total value exceeding $10 billion, signifying their evolution from a proof-of-concept to a functional infrastructure. According to NS3.AI, Circle's USYC has surpassed BlackRock's BUIDL to become the largest tokenized Treasury product. This change is attributed to better distribution, simpler collateral mechanics, and wider investor access, rather than brand strength. The development underscores the growing significance of integration and operational efficiency in the tokenized collateral sector and indicates a rising adoption by institutional users.
